data.content:
The build fails because the generated file isn't added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit `**/*.cs` glob expands at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so files created during the build are invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` and `<FileWrites>` in the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The problem is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. 

In MSBuild, globs are expanded at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file won't be picked up by the implicit `**/*.cs` glob. The target needs to explicitly add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group so the compiler knows about it.

Additionally, the file should be registered with `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.


I need to modify the target to include the generated file in the Compile items and register it for cleanup.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to the compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target creates `GeneratedInfo.cs` during the build, but never adds it to the `<Compile>` item group. MSBuild's implicit `**/*.cs` glob expands at evaluation time (before any targets execute), so files created later are invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="..."/>` and `<FileWrites Include="..."/>` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` after the file is written. This ensures the compiler sees the file and `dotnet clean` removes it.
